Behavioral_Analysis.Target_Behavior: Active consumption of a Psychobiotic and prebiotic-rich diet (fermented foods, high fiber) to restore gut health
Leverage_Points: Intervention 1: Modifying Capability by providing structured, low-effort psychobiotic and prebiotic meal plans or supplements that bypass the cognitive fatigue and physical lethargy associated with Sickness Behavior.

Intervention 2: Modifying Motivation by reframing dietary cravings through education on the Gut-Brain Axis, helping individuals cognitively separate their own reflective goals from the automatic, pathogenic drives of a dysbiotic Microbiota.

Behavioral_Analysis.Target_Behavior: Avoidance of Microbiota-harming agents (e.g., unnecessary broad-spectrum antibiotics, high-sugar processed foods, and emulsifiers)
Leverage_Points: Intervention 1: Modifying Opportunity by restructuring medical environments to default to Phage Therapy, targeted bacteriocins, or Fecal Microbial Transplants instead of broad-spectrum antibiotics, structurally preserving the patient's commensal microbes.

Intervention 2: Modifying Motivation by implementing non-dietary stress-reduction protocols (e.g., targeted exercise) to down-regulate the Hypothalamic-Pituitary-Adrenal Axis, directly reducing the automatic neuroendocrine drive to consume sugary, inflammation-promoting comfort foods.
